prompt_text:
I'm going to describe two scenarios. In the memory scenario, a novel object called a moob has a property: it can be activated by pressing one of its buttons. Your job is to figure out which object in the perception scenario is the moob-analog, and therefore which button on it can be activated.

Memory scenario: There are 6 objects on a table: a moob, a nop, a xav, a rask, a tunk, and a twix. The moob is underneath the nop. The nop is to-the-left-of the xav. The xav is above the rask. The rask is to-the-right-of the tunk. The tunk is underneath the twix. The moob has a green button on top, a blue button on side, and a yellow button on bottom. The nop has a blue button on top and a green button on side. The xav has a blue button on top and a green button on bottom. The rask has a blue button on top and a green button on bottom. The tunk has a blue button on top and a green button on bottom. The twix has a yellow button on top and a green button on back. Pressing the green button on the moob activates it.

Perception scenario: There are 6 objects on a shelf: a dask, a nurp, a zant, a ikk, a brall, and a zop. The nurp is underneath the zop. The zop is to-the-left-of the zant. The zant is above the ikk. The ikk is to-the-right-of the dask. The dask is underneath the brall. The dask has a blue button on top and a yellow button on bottom. The nurp has a green button on top, a blue button on side, and a yellow button on bottom. The zant has a blue button on top and a yellow button on bottom. The ikk has a blue button on top and a blue button on bottom. The brall has a yellow button on top and a blue button on bottom. The zop has a blue button on top, a green button on side, and a yellow button on bottom.

Which object in the perception scenario is the moob-analog, and which button activates it?
